label: Toe sprain with negative x-ray and no open wound or diabetic-foot risk
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of toe sprain/strain.
X-ray performed and negative, with intact neurovascular exam and no open wound, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Fracture or dislocation concern, open wound, nail-bed injury requiring separate pathway, infection, neurovascular compromise, severe pain, inability to bear weight, high-energy trauma, or crush injury.
Diabetic-foot risk, neuropathy, PVD, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed podiatry/orthopedic plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, sports medicine, podiatry, or orthopedics if pain, walking, or swelling is not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports sprain/strain framing, protection/rest/elevation concepts, gradual return to activity, and escalation for worsening pain, swelling, numbness, color change, fever, inability to move, or inability to walk. Autonomous Phase 2001-2100 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed toe sprain/strain with negative x-ray, intact neurovascular exam, and no open wound, infection, diabetic-foot/PVD/neuropathy risk, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of toe sprain/strain
no open wound, infection, diabetic-foot risk, or specialist-directed plan
